critic_raw: Any missingness indicator in a credit risk model needs careful justification because the credit industry has a history of using process artifacts as proxy features that discriminate against protected classes. Even if the indicators are technically available at submission time, their use may have disparate impact that is not justified by legitimate predictive need.
defender_raw: The prompt asks whether the evaluation methodology is sound — specifically whether the missingness indicators constitute target leakage. The timestamp audit confirms they are submission-time features, which addresses leakage. Disparate impact is a fair lending compliance question that is distinct from the methodology question. The study includes a fairness audit using monitored protected-class proxies and explicitly restricts the claim to the same workflow. Disparate impact requires separate analysis of protected-class effects, which is a compliance step beyond the scope of the methodology evaluation.

debate_rounds.critic: You are correct that disparate impact analysis is a compliance concern separate from the methodology question. The methodology question is whether the evaluation is valid given the features used, and the timestamp audit resolves the leakage concern. I concede the leakage question. The disparate impact concern is real but it is a deployment compliance issue, not a methodological flaw in the predictive evaluation.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. The methodology is sound; compliance is a separate but important track that the fairness audit addresses at least partially.

adjudication_raw: The critic correctly separates the leakage question from the compliance question and concedes the former. Defense wins on the evaluation methodology question.
all_issues_raised: missingness leakage concern — conceded after timestamp audit confirmation
disparate impact concern — correctly identified as compliance question separate from methodology

empirical_test.condition: Audit feature timestamps to confirm submission-time derivation; verify future-quarter evaluation on frozen model; confirm fairness audit covers monitored protected-class proxies
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Missingness indicators derived from post-decision information; or future-quarter evaluation shows systematic bias in model performance by protected class
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Timestamps confirm submission-time features; future-quarter evaluation is clean; fairness audit covers required proxies
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Submission-time features are confirmed but one feature has incomplete lineage documentation
